sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
INTERESTING! Clicking on the purple top half of the split block changed it! The unframed block went from:
- Before: purple 5×10 + white 5×10 (horizontal split)
- After: purple triangle (55 pixels) + white triangle (45 pixels) (diagonal split!)
The boundary shows:
- Purple: [[34, 27], [34, 36], [43, 36]] - this is a triangle
- White: [[35, 27], [43, 35], [43, 27]] - this is also a triangle
So clicking on one half of the split block rotates the split line! It went from a horizontal split to a diagonal split. This is a new mechanic!
The unframed block is now split diagonally with purple in the upper-right triangle and white in the lower-left triangle.
So the mechanics are:
1. Selector buttons: change framed block color
2. SPACE: merge/split the unframed block
3. LEFT/RIGHT: rotate the framed block shape (rectangle↔diamond)
4. Clicking on split halves: rotates the split line direction
This means the unframed block can have different split orientations! Let me try clicking again to see if the split rotates further.

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content:
World model updated: MAJOR DISCOVERY! Clicking on a split half of the unframed block ROTATES the split line! It went from horizontal (purple top + white bottom) to diagonal (purple upper-right triangle + white lower-left triangle). This is a new mechanic!
Plan: Try clicking on the split halves again to see if the split continues rotating through different orientations.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Current: unframed block has diagonal split (purple upper-right, white lower-left)
# Try clicking on the white half to see if split rotates further
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 40, 'col': 30}])  # Click white lower-left triangle
print("After clicking white half:")
seg = current_frame.segmentation
for node in seg['nodes']:
    if node['pixels'] > 30 and node['color'] in ['W', 'p', 'g']:
        print(f"  {node['color']} pixels={node['pixels']} boundary={node['boundary']}")

# Try clicking on purple half
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 36, 'col': 34}])  # Click purple upper-right triangle
print("\nAfter clicking purple half:")
seg = current_frame.segmentation
for node in seg['nodes']:
    if node['pixels'] > 30 and node['color'] in ['W', 'p', 'g']:
        print(f"  {node['color']} pixels={node['pixels']} boundary={node['boundary']}")

# Try clicking on white half again
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 40, 'col': 30}])
print("\nAfter clicking white half again:")
seg = current_frame.segmentation
for node in seg['nodes']:
    if node['pixels'] > 30 and node['color'] in ['W', 'p', 'g']:
        print(f"  {node['color']} pixels={node['pixels']} boundary={node['boundary']}")

# Try clicking on purple half again
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 36, 'col': 34}])
print("\nAfter clicking purple half again:")
seg = current_frame.segmentation
for node in seg['nodes']:
    if node['pixels'] > 30 and node['color'] in ['W', 'p', 'g']:
        print(f"  {node['color']} pixels={node['pixels']} boundary={node['boundary']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
